summary: A superb sound‑board transfer of a 1979 show that sizzles from start to finish, highlighted by a ferocious Shakedown > Bertha, a monster The Other One and a killer Jack Straw.
review: The Charlie Miller (CM) sbd rip is crisp, balanced and virtually hiss‑free, delivering an immersive listening experience. Musically the night is a powerhouse – the first set rolls strong with Jack Straw and Tennessee Jed while the second set explodes on Shakedown > Bertha, The Other One, Big River and Lost Sailor > Saint, earning rave praise from most reviewers. A few listeners note Jerry’s playing waned toward the end, but overall sentiment is overwhelmingly positive.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, the majority 4‑5 stars, repeatedly praising both the recording fidelity and the performance; only one low (3‑star) dissenting view.

recording_quality.technical_notes: CM’s transfer is described as crisp, balanced with great instrument separation. A few early comments mention a faint hiss and a patched cut in Bertha, but later reviewers confirm the hiss is gone and the patch seamless.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds solid momentum with tight renditions of Jack Straw and Tennessee Jed; the second set dives deep into improvisation, linking Shakedown into Bertha seamlessly and delivering a climactic Other One. Transitions are praised as smooth and energetic throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Most reviewers celebrate Jerry being ‘in the zone’, especially on Shakedown > Bertha and Lost Sailor > Saint, though one critic felt his guitar phrasing slipped later in the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass receives glowing remarks – described as deep, bomb‑dropping and a major driver of the show’s power.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob is noted for solid rhythm work and even taking lead on Truckin' during the early Brent era.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s organ shines on Shakedown > Bertha; reviewers highlight his funky voicings, vocal harmonies and fresh high‑register tone.
